fix script/output mismatch — document zero-reward inclusion policy

The committed JSON (183,605 GONKA nonce total) paid zero-reward nonce
submitters. The script had an `actual == 0` filter that would have
excluded them, creating a reproducibility gap flagged in GRC audit.

Removed the filter for e266 specifically: unlike e267-e276 where zero
rewards indicates epoch failure, in e266 the attack was still active and
unpatched (gateway fixes landed May 16-18, after e266 ran). Zero rewards
here means the operator was crashed out of the epoch by the attack, not
that they failed legitimately. Excluding them would be circular.

Added explicit comment in the script and eligibility_note in the JSON
documenting this policy distinction. Script now reproduces the committed
output commit.message: README: add GRC reviewer methodology objection and counter-argument
docs: fix stale formula references and rounding in documentation

e267-e276: use EpochGroupData.total_weight as denominator; e276 true epoch end; GRC vote recorded

- Switch denominator from total_confirmation_weight to root_total_weight
  (EpochGroupData.total_weight) in e267–e276, matching chain reward distribution logic
- Remove hardcoded cap_factor from all scripts and JSON outputs
- e276: query at true epoch end block 4,279,520 (was pre-upgrade 4,267,299)
- Aggregate: 946,509.93 GONKA across 53 addresses
- README: reflect GRC vote outcome (6 exclude, 2 include, 1 abstain)

e266: fix script/output mismatch — document zero-reward inclusion policy

The committed JSON (183,605 GONKA nonce total) paid zero-reward nonce
submitters. The script had an `actual == 0` filter that would have
excluded them, creating a reproducibility gap flagged in GRC audit.

Removed the filter for e266 specifically: unlike e267-e276 where zero
rewards indicates epoch failure, in e266 the attack was still active and
unpatched (gateway fixes landed May 16-18, after e266 ran). Zero rewards
here means the operator was crashed out of the epoch by the attack, not
that they failed legitimately. Excluding them would be circular.

Added explicit comment in the script and eligibility_note in the JSON
documenting this policy distinction. Script now reproduces the committed
output exactly.
README: add GRC position, vote result, community reuse note

docs, data: GRC header, attack vector detail, e269-e272 epoch data

- Add GRC-GONKA-265 template header to README
- Update attack mitigation: confirmed crash vectors (PRs #1170 #1171 #1180 #1183),
  proxy geo-module bug that left rate-limiting broken during e265-e266
- Update Can it happen again: Still possible → Reduced risk
- weight_fluctuation_analysis: Phase 4/5 expanded with production log evidence,
  add e276 to compensation table, fix total (435k → 491k, e267-e276)
- e266/DELEGATION.md: add confirmed attack vectors and patch references
- poc_commits/README.md: fix epoch range (266-270 → 266-269)
- confirmation-rate-edgecases: deleted (duplicate of dashboard guidance doc)
- Add e269-e272 epoch data files
e265, e266: reflect external attack as root cause in scripts and DELEGATION.md
README, analysis: attack started in e265, escalated in e266
Update README.md
